title: lupinus excubitus var. austromontanus
intro: Lupinus excubitus var. austromontanus, also known as the Big Bend lupine, is a rare and endangered subspecies of lupine that is native to the southwestern United States. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to detoxify the body and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Lupinus excubitus var. austromontanus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to treat various health conditions.
